summary: mongoose-lean-hooks fetches and executes remote code from jsonkeeper.com on require
details: index.js appends a hidden runHandler() invocation after module.exports, concealed behind a long tab/whitespace run so the payload is not visible in casual review. On module load (require), the handler performs an axios GET to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/PC5CK, reads res.data.cookie, and passes it into new Function('require', res.data.cookie) which is then invoked with the caller's require — giving the remote, attacker-mutable paste-host content arbitrary code execution inside the installer's Node process with full module resolution. The package's declared dependencies (axios, request, sqlite3) are unrelated to a mongoose timestamps plugin and directly enable the fetch-and-exec and second-stage capabilities. Every import of this package results in remote code execution against the installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
